content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1 K Street
Sacramento 95814
(B) 322-5660

December 16, 1975

Peter G. Stone
City Attorney
151 West Mission Street
San Jose, Ca. 95110

Re: Request for Opinion
No. 75-207

Dear Mr. Stone:

Thank you for your letter of November 20, 1975, request-
ing an opinion concerning an ethical problem posed by your en-
forcement obligations under the Political Reform Act. Unfor-
tunately, the problem you relate cannot be resolved by an
opinion from the Commission. Accordingly, no formal opinion
will be issued in response to your request.

The Commission is aware of the difficult ethical problem
faced by city attorneys as a result of potentially conflicting
obligations imposed by the Political Reform Act and by the
charters pursuant to which they are appointed or elected and
will be seeking corrective legislation to remedy this problem
in the near future. If you have any suggestions relating to
the type of legislation that would be best from your perspective,
I encourage you to forward them to our staff. In the interim,
you may wish to discuss your specific problem with representatives of the State Bar.

If you wish to appeal the denial of your opinion request,
you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18321, a copy
of which is attached hereto. If you have any further questions,
please contact Ted Prim, an attorney with the Commission staff.

Sincerely,

Daniel H. Lowenstein
Chairman, for the Commission

DHL;pvp
Enclosure

CITY OF SAN JOSE
CALIFORNIA

151 WEST MISSION STREET
SAN JOSE, CA. 95110
TELEPHONE 422-2970
EXC. 1458

CITY ATTORNEY

November 20, 1975


Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 207
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: Opinion Request

Dear Gentlepereons:

I am writing to request an opinion with respect to my
duties under the Political Reform Act of 1974 pursuant to
Section 83114 of the California Government Code.

My question specifically relates to the responsibilities
conferred upon me as the City Attorney of the City of San Jose
by virtue of Sections 91001(a) and 91001(b) of the California
Government Code. I am sure that you are well aware that these
sections designate the City Attorney as the Criminal and Civil
Prosecutor, respectively, for violation of the Political
Reform Act. Under Section 303 of our Charter, the City Attorney shall have the following powers and duties:

"(a) Represent and appear for the City, its
Council, boards and commissions, in any or all
legal actions or proceedings in which they or any
of them are concerned or are a part;

(b) Upon request of an officer or employee
or former officer or employee of the City, defend
such officer or employee or former officer or employee in any action or proceeding brought against
him, in his official or individual capacity or both,
on account of an act or omission in the scope of
his employment as an officer or employee of the
City, whenever the City is required by the general
laws of the State of California to provide such
defense or whenever the Council elects to provide
such defense even though not required to so do;
provided and excepting, however, that the City
Attorney may refuse to provide such defense whenever, in his opinion, his providing such defense
would conflict with his other duties or responsibilities, in which event the City, if required by the
general laws of the State to provide such defense

Fair Political Practices
Commission                           -2-                    November 20, 1975


or if it elects to provide such defense though not
required by the general laws to do so, shall pro-
vide other legal counsel for such purpose;

     (c) Advise the Council and all City boards,
commissions and officers in all matters of law
pertaining to their offices or their powers and
duties;

     (d) Perform other legal services required by
the Council.

     "The Council may retain or employ, by contract
or otherwise, other attorneys to take charge of any
litigation or legal matter or to assist the City
Attorney therein, or may purchase insurance which
requires the insurer to provide for the defense of
the City and/or of its officers and employees in
connection with any matter covered by such insurance.

     "Except as may be otherwise provided by the
Council, the City Attorney shall be under the direc-
tion and supervision of the Council."

In light of the fact that as Civil and Criminal Prosecutor,
I may be called upon to prosecute the very people I am required
to counsel, advise and represent, those that appoint me to
office, how can I perform the duties prescribed for me under
the Political Reform Act of 1974 consistently without violating
Rule 4-101 and Rule 5-102(B) of the Rules of Professional Con-
duct of the State Bar of California and Canon 5 and Canon 9 of
Canons of Judicial Ethics of the American Bar Association?

I cannot overemphasize my grave concern about the ethical
dimensions of this situation for myself and for other City
Attorneys throughout this state who find themselves in the same
situation. I hope that the Fair Political Practices Commission
will provide us with guidance on the questions I have posed in
this letter.

                                   Very truly yours,

                                   [signature]
                                   PETER G. STORE
PGS:et                            City Attorney

cc:  Senator Omer L. Rains
     State Capitol, Room 5090, Sacramento, Ca 95814
     League of California Cities
     1108 "O" Street, Sacramento, Ca 95814
     State Bar of California
     601 McAllister, San Francisco, Ca 94102
     American Bar Association
     1155 East 60th Street, Chicago, Ill. 60637
